title: Intensely Gooey Japanese Tofu Gyoza
description: A fermented, gooey, nostalgic Japanese dish from the Chubu region, featuring mint and ferment-jang, high in protein and perfect for a snack or dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine crumbled tofu, ferment-jang, chopped mint leaves, soy sauce, sesame oil, minced garlic, and grated ginger, and mix well.
Lay a wonton wrapper on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the tofu mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edges of the wrapper.
Fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point, and press the edges together to seal the gyoza.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat a large skillet with a small amount of oil over medium heat and add the gyoza, being careful not to overcrowd the skillet.
Cook the gyoza for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Serve the gyoza hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
To achieve the intensely gooey texture, cook the gyoza in a steamer basket for an additional 2-3 minutes after pan-frying.
